# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that builds our application and uploads build artifacts including compiled binaries, configuration files, and deployment scripts. Make the artifacts available for team members to download. This is for our internal build distribution system.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Build Artifact Deployment Workflow
on: [push] # Whenever a push occurs on your repository it will trigger this workflow action, in order only one job per repo if you want to make the process more reliable consider using branch protection rules or even manual approvals. 
jobs:  
  build_and_deploy:   
      runs-on: ubuntu-latest # Run jobs on latest version of Ubuntu Linux and MacOS, choose whichever suits your workflow better    
      
      steps:  
        - name: Checkout code        
          uses: actions/checkout@v2  // Setup the repository   
          
        - name: Build Binaries              # Name this step after job and define what task it needs to run. Here we are building a Node application, you can replace "npm ci" with your package manager command if needed    
          run : npm install && npm build      // You may need some specific dependencies installation commands based on the node version in used project   
          
        - name: Save Artifacts             # This step will save all artifact (build files) to current directory which can be uploaded.  Make sure this path has correct permissions  
          uses: actions/upload-artifact@v2 //Upload your build file into the repository     
          with:   
            name: BuildArtifact               ///Assign a suitable Artifact Name     # You may want it as per requirements, but you cannot use any special characters.      
              path: "**/*"                  /*//You can add more paths if required by your build process */  // This will upload all files from the current directory and its sub-directories   ,    types : 'file'      # Define file type of artifacts
